title: Kripapureeswarar Temple
tagline: Kripapureeswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kripapureeswarar Temple (கிருபாபுரீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) (also called Arutkondanathar or Thiruvennainallur temple) in Thiruvennainallur, a panchayat town in Villupuram district in the Sou
overview.description: Kripapureeswarar Temple (கிருபாபுரீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) (also called Arutkondanathar or Thiruvennainallur temple) in Thiruvennainallur, a panchayat town in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanmars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam. The temple is closely associated with Sundarar, the saivite saint of the 8th century, who started composing his Tirumurai starting with "Pitha Piraisudi" verse in this t

topPlaces.name: Thiruvennainallur
topPlaces.description: Thiruvennainallur is a taluk in Viluppuram district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a major sugarcane agricultural region.

topPlaces.name: Koovagam
topPlaces.description: Koovagam (கூவாகம்) is a village in the Ulundurpettai taluk in Kallakurichi district, Tamil Nadu. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Viluppuram and 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Ulundurpet.

topPlaces.name: Sirumadurai
topPlaces.description: Sirumadurai is a village located at Thiruvennainallur block of Villupuram district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is a part of Tirukkoyilur state Assembly Constituency.

topPlaces.name: Sivalokanathar temple, Gramam
topPlaces.description: Sivalokanathar temple (also called Thirumundeeswaram or Sivalokanatha temple, Gramam) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Gramam, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Sivalokanathar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Marundeeswarar Temple, T. Edayar
topPlaces.description: Marudeeswarar Temple (மருதீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) (also called Idayatrunathar Temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in T. Edaiyar, a village in Viluppuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Marudeeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Arasur
topPlaces.description: Arasur is a revenue village in the Thiruvennainallur taluk of Viluppuram district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and the headquarters of Arasur revenue block.

topPlaces.name: Kappur
topPlaces.description: Kappur is a Village Panchayat in Villupuram District, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It serves as the headquarters of Kappur Panchayat which includes Nerkunam, Kuchipalyam, Govindapuram.

items.note: Thiruvennainallur is a taluk in Viluppuram district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a major sugarcane agricultural region.
Koovagam (கூவாகம்) is a village in the Ulundurpettai taluk in Kallakurichi district, Tamil Nadu. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Viluppuram and 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) from Ulundurpet.
Sirumadurai is a village located at Thiruvennainallur block of Villupuram district in Tamil Nadu, India. It is a part of Tirukkoyilur state Assembly Constituency.

Sivalokanathar temple (also called Thirumundeeswaram or Sivalokanatha temple, Gramam) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Gramam, a village in Villupuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Sivalokanathar, and is represented by the lingam.

Marudeeswarar Temple (மருதீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) (also called Idayatrunathar Temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in T. Edaiyar, a village in Viluppuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Marudeeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.
Arasur is a revenue village in the Thiruvennainallur taluk of Viluppuram district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, and the headquarters of Arasur revenue block.
Kappur is a Village Panchayat in Villupuram District, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It serves as the headquarters of Kappur Panchayat which includes Nerkunam, Kuchipalyam, Govindapuram.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kripapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kripapureeswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kripapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (141 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (139 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kripapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kripapureeswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kripapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kripapureeswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kripapureeswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiruvennainallur, Koovagam, Sirumadurai, Sivalokanathar temple, Gramam, Marundeeswarar Temple, T. Edayar.
